sections.heading: Prepositions of Place
sections.body:
in — inside something: in the room, in the box, in Delhi
on — on a surface: on the table, on the wall, on the bus
at — specific point: at school, at home, at the door
between — in the middle of two: between A and B
among — in the middle of many: among friends
next to — beside: next to the library
in front of — before: in front of the building
behind — at the back: behind the house

sections.heading: Prepositions of Time
sections.body:
at — specific time: at 3pm, at noon, at night, at the weekend
on — days/dates: on Monday, on July 17th, on my birthday
in — longer periods: in the morning, in July, in 2026, in summer
for — duration: for 3 hours, for a week, for many years
since — starting point: since Monday, since 2020, since I was a child
until/till — up to: until Friday, till tomorrow
before/after — sequence: before lunch, after work

sections.heading: Conjunctions
sections.body:
Coordinating (connect equal parts):
and, but, or, so, yet
'I like tea and coffee.' / 'It's small but powerful.'

Subordinating (connect unequal parts):
because, although, when, while, if, unless, until
'I stayed home because it rained.'
'Although it was cold, we went outside.'

Correlative (come in pairs):
both...and, either...or, neither...nor, not only...but also
'Both Ali and Sara passed the test.'

jargon.definition: A word showing the relationship between a noun and other words. Examples: in, on, at, by, for, with, to.
A word that connects words, phrases, or clauses. Examples: and, but, because, although, while.
A preposition + its object. Example: 'in the morning' (preposition: in, object: the morning). Functions as an adjective or adverb.

summary: Prepositions show time, place, and relationships. 'In' for enclosed spaces and longer periods, 'on' for surfaces and days, 'at' for specific points and times. Practice with real examples.
